feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Datasphere
feed.subtitle: SAP Datasphere Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Optimizing Data Load for Large Replication Flow Object
entry.content:
Good day All,

I have a quick question regarding the optimization of data loading for a replication flow object containing over 400 million records. The object in question is the CDS view I_GLACCOUNTLINEITEMRAWDATA, which includes 348 columns.

We are using replication flows to transfer data from an on-premise S/4HANA system to Datasphere, utilizing an initial + delta load method.

I started the flow on Wednesday, but it stalled and ultimately failed on Thursday night after loading 170 million records out of the expected 400 million. The flow was stopped and then restarted. It is now presenting the following errors in Datasphere and S/4 HANA

SAP Datasphere:

S4 (Transaction code DHCDCMON )

Does anyone have suggestions or experience with optimizing the load process for such a large data set?

Thank you in advance for your guidance!

SAP Datasphere

SAP S/4HANA

entry.title: Error FWM 02151 Using SAP Business Objects 4.3. The token between Datasphere and BO expire.
entry.content:
A few months ago we connected sap datasphere with bo 4.3 directly without universes in between. For that we configure the token, which does not expire. But every now and then it happens to us that it blocks and the user cannot access the report. What is the reason and what is the permanent solution?

We provisionally found that by accessing bo with the user, we renewed the token and it works

Thanks in advance!!

Natalia.

entry.title: How can you model a Parent-Child Hierarchy with two Key attributes in the Datasphere?
entry.content:
Hello,

My issue is that I have two dimensional tables: Project and Costs. Each project has one or multiple cost codes.

Project

Pr_nrattributes
001x
002xx
003xxx

and Cost

Pr_nrCost_CodeDepBudget
001AFI10000
001BCO5000
002BFI8000

My problem is that some attributes can only be identified by the combined key of Cost_Code and Pr_nr. I am a beginner in this field, so my expertise is limited. I considered merging the dimensions and using the combined key as the identifier, but my problem still stands. Due to the combined key, I need to build a parent-child hierarchy.

Any help is much appreciated. Thank you in advance.

Best regards,

Marco

entry.title: How to integrate SAP Datasphere Consumption API in SAP BTP Destination and SAP BTP-BPA
entry.content:
Hi All,

I am facing an issue when creating a destination on Sap data sphere Consumption API. I am getting a 401 authorization issue when creating a Destination using Oauth 2.0. However, using the same URL in Postman is working fine.

Can you suggest a solution to this issue?

This is the authorization I am using for Postman.

entry.title: Hierarchy not brought in via Cloud data integration connection in Datasphere
entry.content: When I tried to bring in the Master Data (from the Dimension Table) via the SAC Connection created in Datasphere using the Cloud Data Integration connection in Datasphere, the Hierarchy is not brought into Datasphere from the Dimension table in SAC Model. Other columns like Member ID and Description are brought in except the Hierarchy column. I tried via both the Remote Tables and Data Flow; the Hierarchy is not brought in from SAC to Datasphere using the Cloud Data Integration (CDI) connection. Can you support on how to bring the Hierarchy using the CDI connection.

entry.title: replicating S/4 VDM models in Datasphere
entry.content:
Hello Friends,

Given that there is a vast array of CDS views and Queries withing SAP S/4 VDM, is it necessary to recreate similar models in Datasphere?  It sounds like a duplication of effort.  What do you all think?  What would be the best practice in this regard?  As you know, SAP SAC provides live connection to S/4 for reporting utilizing the vast array of built-in CDS Analytical Queries.   There must be a blog on this subject, but I cannot find it.  Let me know if you know of any blogs on this subject.

Thanks

Abdul

entry.title: How to Replace 0 with Blank in SAP Datasphere and SAC
entry.content:
Hi Community,

I’m working in SAP Datasphere and trying to implement a solution where the "Subtotal Amt" field shows a blank instead of 0. However, even after applying a calculated column using the following formula:

CASE WHEN "Subtotal Amt" = 0 THEN NULL ELSE "Subtotal Amt"

The system still shows 0 instead of a blank value in Analytic Model and SAC.

I’m looking for a way to display a true blank (or NULL) for 0 values in SAP Datasphere, so that it also reflects correctly in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) reports.

What I’ve Tried:

  • Creating calculated columns with NULL.
  • Ensuring data model updates and SAC model refreshes.
  • Attempting conditional formatting in SAC, which did not solve the issue either.

Has anyone successfully implemented a solution where numeric 0 values are replaced with blanks? Any help or suggestions would be greatly appreciated!

entry.title: Open Connectors with Datasphere "may not work"
entry.content:
Hello,

How can I know in advance if an Open Connector will work with Datasphere before buying the Integration Suite?

According to this note https://me.sap.com/notes/0003445022

Not all Open Connectors work with datasphere!!

And from SAP Help page page https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_DATASPHERE/be5967d099974c69b77f4549425ca4c0/9bfe7db51216449d985a0b59f5e181c4.html?locale=en-US

We can see that the data extraction is always done with a full retrieve of the source without filter/parameter push-down capability.

By the way, entering in the link "Working with Connectors".

gives no usefull answers https://help.openconnectors.ext.hana.ondemand.com/home/working-with-elements

 

Thanks.

entry.title: Improve Third Party Apps Data Consumption from Datasphere
entry.content:
Hello experts,

We have been experimenting long data retrieval durations when consuming views created in SAP Datasphere in Third Party apps like Excel and Power BI.

We consume data via ODBC Connection (changing this is not an option for us), so we use the Database user from each space we wish to consume data in order to make a successful connection.

Every graphical view or SQL view created, uses as data sources remote tables. We work through data federation as we cannot store data on the cloud due to security politics. The remote tables have as main source On-premise SAP HANA Warehouse, connected to Datasphere through a DPA (Data Provisioning Agent).

We have various cases, some of them, we identified the SQL query is complex and that's why it takes a long time.

But other cases, when the SQL Query is simple, but the number of rows is high (600,000) we suppose we should have a better time when retrieving that data (as with other tools the time is considerable smaller).

Is there any parameter we could modify in the DPA configuration, HANA configuration or in Datasphere, so we can improve the data retrieval times?

Thank you.

entry.title: How to connect to Analytic Model in Power BI
entry.content:
Hello,

 

I am importing a data source in SAP Datasphere with ODBC connector and using it in PBI.

I tried to use Analytic Model from datasphere in PBI.

But calculated and restricted measures created in Analytic Model are not displayed.

Only the fact tables are imported.

 

Is there a way to connect and use the Analytic Model in PBI?

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: SAP Datesphere Error 423 Locked
entry.content:
Hi expert, 

My SAP Datasphere free-tier subscription has expired. I'm receiving below error.

How could I delete the instance of data sphere and recreate it?

Thanks.

Regards,

Ben

entry.title: Connect to azure blob storage using SAS token in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Hi team,

We use BTP Object Store as a service in our applications and it generates SAS token-based credentials for the Blob container. Is there a way to connect to the Blob container using the SAS token in Datasphere? 

We have a few delta tables in the container and want to access them in Datasphere. Based on the help documentation and the connections UI, I can see that it only asks for the shared key but doesn't support any other type of credentials for the Blob container.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_DATASPHERE/be5967d099974c69b77f4549425ca4c0/df5a7c56ac21472c973f6fab341f3991.html

Any leads on this would help us proceed with the proof of concept we are working on. I really appreciate any help you can provide.

entry.title: Looking for guidance to separate Datasphere workspace.
entry.content:
Hi team,

Hope you are well today. For those using SAP Datasphere or the older version productively, how do you logically separate your workspace? Do you separate it out by functional area e.g. MM, SD, FI or do you separate it out by business e.g. Finance, Supply Chain, HR etc or do you separate spaces based on Inbound Layer, Harmonization Layer, Reporting Layer or do you separate/create new spaces based on use case e.g. a use case to solve a data science problem and another use case to build operational reporting

Ideally, I think it should separate it out by functional area e.g MM, SD, FI and have a dedicated workspace just for master data which can be shared across the different workspace.

Would be interested to know what are people doing out there that is working for them.

Thank you for your time.

entry.title: How to create Row level Security on user groups in Datasphere
entry.content:
Hey Specs,

I've two questions.

I am digging deep into the Topic of the Row Level security.

I've two problem and I am unable to find blog or documentations on it.

Is there a way to assign the Dac object so the row level security to user groups ? 
It makes no sense to assign row level security users. We have around 300 users. If i need to do excel for each reports for each functional area then that would be to much manualyti so I would like to create roles for row level sec.


Is there a way to create row level security for a list of values like hierarchy nodes or list of distinct values withouth duplicating rows ?  
Now as far as I know if I want give access to multiple values for an user I need to duplicate each user's by the number of distinct values. So if an user can access 300 costcenter then I need to create a table where the user is duplicated by 300 rows for each distinct costcenter. But that would be a nightmare.

entry.title: SCIM 2.0 API - User Provisioning - Bulk Operations (BulkRequest)
entry.content:
Hi All,

Recently started investigating SCIM 2.0 API functionality for Datasphere. I managed to create a single user, as well as delete a user. So authorization wise I should not encounter any issues.

Now for bulk operations following scheme needs to be used: 

"schemas": ["urn:ietf:params:scim:api:messages:2.0:BulkRequest"]

Running this for both the creation of users as well as for the deletion of users, it doens't seem to work. I received a 400 error ("Request is unparsable, syntactically incorrect, or violates schema").

 
Looking a bit deeper into the list of all avaible schemas under ../scim2/schemas, I don't see the BulkRequest schema listed.
 
I did check the /scim2/ServiceProviderConfig and bulk is supported: 
"bulk":{"supported":true,"maxOperations":30,"maxPayloadSize":3072000}
 
Anyone managed to use this BulkRequest schema and if so how?
Thanks in advance!
 
Kind Regards,
Thibault

entry.title: Writing Table data to csv or text file in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Hi Experts, 

We were able to write data to file format in SAP BODS. Similarly, Is there any operator/transform to write data from DB table to file (CSV or Text) in SAP Datasphere? 

 

Appreciate your help.

 

Thank You

entry.title: currency/unit conversion error: Conversion could not determine valid conversion rate for value(s)
entry.content:
Dear SAP Datasphere experts,

I am facing the error "currency/unit conversion error: Conversion could not determine valid conversion rate for value(s)" while trying to create a calculated column in SAP Datasphere for unit conversion

T006 & T006D tables are present in my space (Z_TGT_UOM_DF & Z_TGT_DIMENSION_DF). I have created a data flow and loaded their content into the respective target tables.

If I use a hard coded value in the CONVERT_UNIT expression for SOURCE_UNIT e.g. "KG", it works fine. Please see bellow

Although, if I try to use a source field (ITEMGROSSWEIGHT) for the SOURCE_UNIT, which has exactly the same value as the previously hard coded one (KG) I get the error.

 

Please note that I have exactly one record for testing.

What could be the possible reason?

Many thanks in advance!

entry.title: SAP Datasphere - Do we need to keep using BW Bridge?
entry.content:
Hello guys, I understand best approach to migrate BW data flows to cloud (SAP Datasphere) is using BW Bridge.  One question on that, please, if you can clarify it.

Once we complete the migration to BW Bridge, next step could be recreate the data flows from BW Bridge space into a native Datasphere and stop using Bridge (not mandatory but, eventually). 

Thank you in advance, 

Marcelo

entry.title: Element "timestamp" is not available in my ABAP CDS View ODP Extractor. How to overcome that?
entry.content:
Hello guys,
I have got four date/time fields - the well known "createdAtdate" and "changedAtdate" , and the respective for time "createdTime" and "changedTime". I want to implement some logic - when "changedAt" fields are present my new column timestamp - for delta extractor purposes - to get values from there, otherwise from "createdAt". So far so good, but when I do the annotations there is an error mark...

 

Any ideas what I do wrong? I tried to cast that field, but I am  a little bit unsecured with the syntaxis yet 🙂
I will appreciate any advice!
BR
HH

entry.title: SAP Datasphere: Cannot allocate enough memory:Allocation failed; Reason: booked memory limit reached
entry.content:
Good day,

I have installed the Finance Foundation business content on SAP Datasphere and am attempting to preview data from the GLACCOUNTLINEITEMRAWDATA object (equivalent to ACDOCA), which contains 600 million records. I am working with an analytical model that has variables to limit the data; however, I am encountering an error that states: "Cannot allocate enough memory: Allocation failed; Reason: booked memory limit reached (SAP Note 3191224)." 

I have reviewed the SAP note but am still unable to preview the data. Has anyone else experienced this issue, and how was it resolved? Thank you for your assistance.

 

SAP Datasphere SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA database
